items.title: Zcash Targeting Post-Quantum Crypto Milestone by 2027

items.title: Bitcoin Optech Newsletter #404
items.desc: This week’s newsletter describes possible solutions to node fingerprinting and links to discussion of using public fraud proofs to improve incentives around just-in-time channels. Also included are our regular sections describing notable changes to popular Bitcoin infrastructure software.

items.title: Kraken parent goes for the OCC charter in bid to become a federal crypto bank
items.desc: Payward’s application would add a federally regulated trust company to the Kraken group’s existing Wyoming bank charter and Fed master account.

items.title: Bitcoin Slips Under $80,000 As ETFs Snap Five-Day Inflow Streak
items.desc: Tron, Cardano and Solana led weekly gains among the Top 10, while Bitcoin and Ether lagged as US spot ETF demand cooled on Thursday.

items.title: Bitcoin bulls battle for $80K control as US jobs data delivers surprise
items.desc: Bitcoin traders called a "healthy bullish backtest" as BTC price action kept them guessing over whether $80,000 could be reclaimed.

items.title: On-Chain, In Court: What happened in crypto legal news this week
items.desc: US authorities find an additional $10 million connected to Sam Bankman-Fried, the former CEO of Celsius ditches his legal team and a new law in Washington state bans crypto ATMs.

items.title: ECB's Lagarde’s digital euro warning: Why Europe shouldn’t just copy the U.S. stablecoin model
items.desc: Lagarde warned that large stablecoins like Tether and USDC, which now dominate a $310 billion market, pose financial stability risks and could transmit stress to underlying asset markets during periods of turmoil.

items.title: ECB’s Lagarde Pushes Back on Euro Stablecoins, Warns of ‘Structural Weaknesses’
items.desc: The European Central Bank chief says Europe "knows which port it is sailing to,” and it's not towards a euro stablecoin.

items.title: Bitcoin’s ‘overbought’ signal flashes price top warning with focus on $78K
items.desc: Bitcoin’s 36% rally from $60,000 resulted in the relative strength index flashing a potential top signal not seen since early 2026.

items.desc: Coinbase says Amazon Web Services failures across multiple availability zones caused an extended outage on its platform.

items.title: XRP pushes toward $1.40 as tightening range lowers breakout chances
items.desc: Price moved higher on a late volume burst, with traders watching whether compression near $1.39-$1.40 finally resolves into a larger move.

items.title: Kelp DAO exploit prompts DeFi protocols to rethink oracle providers
items.desc: Solv Protocol and other DeFi projects are migrating to Chainlink infrastructure after the $293 million exploit exposed risks in third-party bridge and oracle setups.

items.title: Bitcoin rally cools as April jobs fails to break macro ceiling with Iran tensions and ETF outflows in play
items.desc: Bitcoin holds near $80,200 after April nonfarm payrolls beat expectations as Iran tensions and ETF outflows check the rally.

items.title: Europe sees ‘hyperconcentration’ of crypto wrench attacks as losses hit $101M
items.desc: Criminal teams behind wrench attacks usually consist of three to five people, often posing as delivery drivers or luring victims into ambushes, said CertiK.

items.title: Australian police seize $4.1M in Bitcoin tied to darknet market
items.desc: NSW Police said the Bitcoin was allegedly linked to illegal darknet marketplace activity involving drugs and weapons.

items.title: XRP New Addresses, Active Supply Plunge Amid Shift to ‘Institutional Rails’
items.desc: New XRP addresses hit multi-year lows, per Glassnode data, as the network shifts from "retail speculation" to institutional usage.

items.title: Stablecoins have their 'permission slip.' Now comes the hard part.
items.desc: Executives from MoonPay, Ripple and Paxos said at Consensus Miami 2026 that regulation has accelerated stablecoin adoption, but infrastructure, privacy and distribution remain major hurdles.

items.title: Coinbase bulls point to crypto legislation and stablecoins after earnings miss
items.desc: Analysts say Coinbase’s long-term growth may depend more on stablecoins and U.S. crypto legislation than a rebound in trading activity.

items.title: Stablecoins won’t strengthen global role of euro, ECB’s Lagarde says
items.desc: ECB President Christine Lagarde said Europe should build tokenized settlement infrastructure anchored by central bank money rather than rely on private stablecoins.

items.title: U.S. added 115K jobs in April, nearly doubling expectations
items.desc: The data comes ahead of what's expected to be a new Federal Reserve chairman taking the reins at the U.S. central bank later this month.

items.title: Revolut users report Bitcoin price glitch showing BTC at 2 cents
items.desc: The apparent pricing error was not reflected across broader crypto markets, suggesting a platform-specific data or display issue.

items.desc: Coinbase missed earnings, but one of its newest businesses is scaling incredibly fast—and Tom Lee may finally be slowing his Ethereum buying.

items.desc: Your day-ahead look for May 8, 2026

items.title: Four signs that show Ethereum's rally is exhausted at $2.4K
items.desc: Several Ethereum metrics suggest the ETH price could see further downside due to reduced demand and weakening network fundamentals.

items.title: Coinbase blames AWS for hours-long crypto trading outage
items.desc: Coinbase says the outage has been fully resolved but that it will investigate the incident fully.

items.title: Mantle tokenholders approve 30K ETH Aave credit facility after rsETH exploit
items.desc: The credit facility would help Aave address bad debt created after the April rsETH exploit strained its WETH market.

items.title: Arbitrum DAO Votes to Unlock $70 Million for Kelp DAO Exploit Relief
items.desc: Arbitrum DAO voted to release the Kelp exploit funds, but a U.S. Court restraining notice has put the approved transfer in legal jeopardy.

items.title: Bitcoin eyes $80K reclaim as traders say these BTC price support levels must hold
items.desc: Bitcoin retagged $80,000 after falling 3% on Iran concerns, while traders flagged important BTC price support levels to preserve next.

items.title: Senator Warren presses Meta over stablecoin trial ahead of 2026 rollout plans
items.desc: Senator Elizabeth Warren questioned Meta over its stablecoin trial, citing Libra risks and 2026 integration plans.

items.title: Tether freezes over $500M of USDT in 30 days, BlockSec data shows
items.desc: BlockSec data shows Tether froze over $500 million in USDT across 370 Ethereum and Tron addresses in 30 days, adding to $1.26 billion frozen in 2025 linked to illicit activity.

items.title: Bitcoin retreats below $80,000, liquidating $300 million in futures bets
items.desc: BTC fell under $80,000 after U.S. strikes in Iran sent oil briefly above $100, triggering liquidations and a shift toward bearish positioning.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs snap 5-day inflow streak as BTC dips under $80K
items.desc: Bitcoin ETFs snap a five-day $1.7 billion inflow streak with $277.5 million of outflows as Bitcoin falls below $80,000 amid sharp intraday volatility.

items.title: AUSTRAC Launches Supervision Campaigns as Australia's Crypto Regulatory Reforms Kick In
items.desc: The campaigns target over-the-counter operators and local exchanges as new laws expand oversight to custody and brokerage services.

items.title: Stablecoin card spend is growing 100% year over year, Rain exec says
items.desc: Stablecoin settlement enables weekend/holiday settlement, reducing trapped capital by over 40%. This improves card economics and financial flexibility for issuers.

items.title: ECB’s Lagarde flags euro-denominated stablecoins as financial stability risk, diverging from Bundesbank stance
items.desc: The ECB President argued that euro-denominated stablecoins risk financial stability and monetary policy, pushing for a digital euro instead.

items.title: Bitcoin shows 2-cent price on Revolut as users report apparent BTC display glitch
items.desc: Screenshots showed BTC briefly far below global market prices, though it remains unclear whether any trades were executed at those levels or whether the move was a display issue.

items.title: South Korea tightens oversight of firms moving crypto overseas: report
items.desc: South Korean authorities are also planning to start imposing a 22% capital gains tax on crypto from January 2027.

items.title: Arbitrum approves $71 Million ETH release despite U.S. seizure fight
items.desc: DAO delegates approved releasing exploit-frozen ETH to an Aave-led recovery effort, though Arbitrum’s governance rules delay any transfer for at least eight days

items.desc: Coinbase resumes trading after hours of AWS-linked disruption, restoring markets following 'Cancel Only' and auction-mode restrictions.

items.title: Taiwan news anchor indicted for allegedly taking crypto payments from China to produce propaganda, bribe soldiers
items.desc: Taiwan prosecutors indicted a news anchor for allegedly accepting crypto payments from an "overseas hostile force" to produce news shows.

items.title: CLARITY Act support carries electoral boost, HarrisX poll finds
items.desc: The poll found 52% of registered voters support the CLARITY Act, with 47% willing to cross party lines for a candidate who backs the bill.

items.title: US sentences ‘laptop farmers’ tied to North Korean IT worker scheme
items.desc: The Justice Department said two men were sentenced for hosting laptops used by North Korean IT workers, bringing the total to eight sentences in five months.

items.title: Arbitrum DAO approves $70 million ETH release for Kelp DAO recovery
items.desc: However, a May 1 court order restricted Arbitrum DAO from moving the recovered funds, prompting Aave to file an emergency motion against it.

items.title: Consensus panelists explain why Perp DEXes remain a tough sell for institutional investors
items.desc: Panelists at Consensus Miami said institutional investors are still largely absent from perp DEXs, citing security risks and KYC friction as key barriers.

items.title: AWS Northern Virginia data center overheats, impacting Coinbase
items.desc: Coinbase said on Friday its markets are being placed in “cancel only” mode but will begin to re-enable trading “shortly.”

items.title: Chaos Labs says oracles secure after attempted ‘nation-state’ wallet attack
items.desc: Chaos Labs said it rotated all keys after the weekend attack attempt and has not detected further suspicious activity.

items.title: Bitcoin slips to $79,000, DOGE leads majors losses as negative funding rates set 10-year record
items.desc: Bitcoin pulled back from this week's $81,500 high after U.S. forces fired on Iranian targets, while crypto futures markets logged their 67th straight day of negative funding rates, the longest streak in a decade per K33 Research.

items.title: Coinbase shares slide on $400M Q1 loss, revenue miss
items.desc: Coinbase financial chief Alesia Haas said “macro conditions were genuinely tough,” while CEO Brian Armstrong highlighted a plan to diversify beyond spot trading.

items.title: CVE-2024-52911 - Script Interpreter Remote Crash
items.desc: After Bitcoin Core 0.14.0 and before Bitcoin Core 29.0, validating a specially-crafted block may cause the node to access previously freed memory. During validation, necessary data required for checking inputs for each transaction is pre-calculated and cached. For specially crafted invalid blocks, it was possible for this data to be destroyed while it was still being accessed by a background validation thread. An attacker capable of mining a block with sufficient proof-of-work could have exploited this to crash victim nodes. Because of the nature of use-after-free bugs, it is possible that the crash could have been used for remote code execution, though constraints on the input (block) data make this unlikely. This issue is considered High severity. Details By default, script validation for new blocks is dispatched to background threads via a vector of CScriptCheck functors. Each CScriptCheck holds a pointer to a PrecomputedTransactionData object which stores some data needed by each input in the transaction. Because it stores a pointer and not the data itself, care must be taken to ensure that the PrecomputedTransactionData outlives the CScriptCheck. The script checks lifetime is enforced by an RAII class, CCheckQueueControl. However, the control is intantiated before the precomputed transaction data. Because local objects in C++ are destructed in reverse order of construction, this means the vector of PrecomputedTransactionData is destroyed before the CCheckQueueControl. This is not an issue when the block is valid, as CCheckQueueControl::Wait() will be called before the function returns and the PrecomputedTransactionData gets destroyed. However, in case of an early return (when a separate check fails) a background script thread may read the precomputed transaction data after it was destroyed. An attacker could exploit this to crash victim nodes at the expense of a valid PoW at tip. Attribution Cory Fields (MIT DCI) discovered this vulnerability and responsibly disclosed it in a detailed report containing a proof of concept for reproduction and a proposed mitigation. Timeline 2024-11-02 Cory Fields privately reports the bug 2024-11-06 Pieter Wuille pushes a covert fix to already open PR #31112 which works around the issue by removing the early returns 2024-12-03 PR #31112 is merged 2025-04-12 Bitcoin Core version 29.0 is released with a fix 2026-04-19 The last vulnerable Bitcoin Core version (28.x) goes end of life 2026-05-05 Public disclosure.

items.title: Solv Protocol drops LayerZero in favor of Chainlink for $700 million tokenized bitcoin

items.desc: The firm has deployed $216 million to acquire approximately 7.3 million HYPE tokens since its inception in December 2025.

items.title: Bitcoin bulls target $115K by December: Does data back the expectation?
items.desc: Bitcoin options show bulls targeting $115,000 by year-end but are traders becoming overly optimistic?

items.desc: US Treasury officials reportedly sent a letter to Binance pressing the crypto exchange on compliance with a 2023 deal, after reports circulated that the company had facilitated transactions linked to Iran.

items.title: Solv Protocol Will Dump LayerZero, Migrate $700M Tokenized Bitcoin Tech to Chainlink
items.desc: Solv Protocol will move its tokenized Bitcoin infrastructure to Chainlink, following Kelp DAO's lead after it blamed LayerZero for a hack.

items.title: CLARITY Act markup could happen as early as next week: Coinbase exec
items.desc: The provisions in the crypto market structure bill are still under review by the banking and crypto lobbies as a new poll shows bipartisan voter support for the legislation.

items.desc: The company is transforming from a "spot-focused crypto platform to a place" where users can trade many asset classes.

items.desc: Cash App's bitcoin business was down 31% year-over-year, while Square reported a negligible gross profit impact from crypto.

items.title: Privacy and accountability can coexist onchain, say panelists at Consensus Miami
items.desc: Public blockchains make transactions traceable, but that transparency can come at the expense of user privacy. Speakers from Moody’s Ratings and ChangeNOW said that hybrid blockchain architecture and address-level monitoring can help solve both problems simultaneously.

items.title: Tom Lee Says BitMine May Slow Ethereum Buys After Amassing Nearly $12 Billion of ETH
items.desc: Leading Ethereum treasury firm BitMine Immersion Technologies may start slowing its ETH purchases as it approaches its 5% supply goal.

items.title: JPMorgan says bitcoin is gaining over gold as debasement trade after Iran conflict
items.desc: Investors are increasingly choosing bitcoin over gold as a debasement trade since the Iran conflict began, JPMorgan analysts said.

items.title: Tydro Keeps Markets Paused After Chaos Labs Flags Suspected Nation-State Attack
items.desc: The largest DeFi protocol on Kraken's Ink Layer 2 network is onboarding Chainlink and RedStone feeds before resuming its lending markets.

items.desc: The Nasdaq-listed fund offers exposure to Canton Coin, native token of the privacy-focused institutional blockchain network.

items.desc: Bitcoin briefly fell below $80,000 on Thursday as analysts say profit taking has increased among crypto traders.

items.desc: The deal is payable in cash and stock and values Payward at $20 billion.

items.desc: Bitwise will become the investment manager of USCC on June 1, marking its first tokenized fund and Superstate's second handoff to a major asset manager this year.

items.desc: Spot Bitcoin ETFs hit $108.76 billion in net assets as five weeks of inflows and fading put skew signal institutional demand is back.

items.desc: Senate Banking Committee markup is planned for this month, leaving four working Senate weeks in June for floor passage, said Patrick Witt.

items.title: Morning Minute: Morgan Stanley Is Coming for Coinbase
items.desc: Morgan Stanley just launched crypto trading on ETrade, while the White House wants the Clarity Act signed by July 4.

items.title: Why eBay Should Ignore GameStop and Use Bitcoin to Save $1.2 Billion in Transaction Costs
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Why eBay Should Ignore GameStop and Use Bitcoin to Save $1.2 Billion in Transaction Costs Why eBay should reject GameStop and launch Bitcoin payments: A $1.2B annual opportunity cost that returns massive fee savings directly to global sellers. This post Why eBay Should Ignore GameStop and Use Bitcoin to Save $1.2 Billion in Transaction Costs first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Nick Ward.

items.title: Gnosis Treasury Redemption Vote Swings as Whale Counters Cofounder
items.desc: Votes in favor of a redemption proposal that would let GNO holders claim roughly $170 per token from a $223M treasury have retaken the lead on Snapshot.

items.title: Kraken Launches Spot Margin Trading for US Clients
items.desc: Retail users can now post crypto as collateral and trade with up to 10x leverage on Kraken Pro.

items.title: Bitcoin Core 31.0 released
items.desc: Bitcoin Core version 31.0 is now available for download. See the release notes for more information about the bug fixes in this release. If you have any questions, please stop by the #bitcoin IRC chatroom (IRC, web) and we’ll do our best to help you.
